genre_preference.value: dislikes adult themes, comedy, and remakes
evidence: "It's just a dumb idea. What’s next? Remake Godfather?"
"It's not appropriate for kids. One episode has the sheep drunk at a rave."
"The writing is no better - it flows like mud and comes across like an amateur work across the board."
"If I want to hear an old man talk, I'll read a book out loud."
"One of the easiest ways to spot a bad director is reliance on a narrator."
"The director tells rather than shows the story, which means the director doesn't know how to make a film."
"very disappointing film from the Coen brothers."
"They put so many nasty sounds in this - high pitch feedback, weird thunder sounds between scenes."
"It's just a piece of fiction, so why try to make it a 'parody'?"
"It's not clever and they sure shouldn't have spent the money on it."

summary: The user has a strong aversion to adult themes and poorly executed remakes, often criticizing narrative simplicity and pacing. They value high production quality but express disappointment in many films, indicating a preference for well-crafted stories over entertainment value.
